author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅sama nāhi āna
dharma⋅karma⋅tapa⋅tyāga, dhyāna⋅jñāna⋅vrata⋅yāga,
kichu nahe nāmera samāna

je nāma loite hara, preme matta digambara,
vālmīki hoila tapodhana
ajāmila pāpī chila, nāmābhāse tare gela,
putrake ḍākiyā nārāyaṇa

je⋅nāmera svāda peye, tambure phiraye geye,
devoṛṣi nārada⋅gosāi
satya-bhāmā vrata⋅chale, kṛṣṇa⋅saṅge kari’ tūle,
dekhāilā nāmera baḍāi

ananta sahasra mukhe, je nāma gāyena sukhe,
tabu to karite nāre sīmā
lakṣya kari’ arjunake, prabhu āpanāra mukhe,
kahechena nāmera mahimā

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ bala anukṣaṇa,
durvāsanā chāḍiyā hṛdaya
preme ucca nāma kari’, avaśya pāibe hari,
nāma āra nāmī bhinna naya
en_translation:
O my mind! There is nothing equal to the Name of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Religious duties, pious activities, penance, renunciation, meditation, knowledge, vows, and sacrifices—none of these are equal to the Holy Name.

By taking that Name, Lord Śiva became intoxicated with love, and Vālmīki became a great sage. Ajāmila was a great sinner, but he was delivered even by a shadow of the Name when he called for his son, Nārāyaṇa.

Having tasted the sweetness of that Name, the divine sage Nārada Muni wanders about, singing while playing his vīṇā. Under the guise of a vow, Satyabhāmā weighed Lord Kṛṣṇa against the Holy Name and demonstrated its supreme greatness.

Lord Ananta Śeṣa, with His thousands of mouths, happily sings that Name, yet He can never reach its end. Addressing Arjuna, the Lord Himself spoke of the glory of the Name with His own mouth.

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' constantly, abandoning all evil desires from your heart. By loudly chanting the Name with love, you will surely attain Lord Hari, for the Name and the Lord are not different."
